span.p:
Compiled callable — PyTensor's output
θ (a point in parameter space) → logp(θ), ∇logp(θ)
(observed data baked in as constants)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Sampler loop:
propose θ → call the compiled logp/∇logp → accept/reject → repeat (many times)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
PyMC NUTS
pure-Python loop
(calls a callable from any CPU backend, e.g. C / Numba)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
nutpie / nuts-rs
Rust loop
(needs a Numba or JAX callable)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
NumPyro NUTS
JAX loop
(needs a JAX callable)

div.style: color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
BlackJAX NUTS
JAX loop
(needs a JAX callable)
